classObjective: Find the inverse of a relation given its graph, table, or set of ordered pairs and find the inverse of a linear function algebraically using the switch-and-solve method.
workedExamples: 5-6, Example 1 — Find the Inverse from a Graph or Table
5-6, Example 2 — Find the Inverse from Ordered Pairs
5-6, Example 3 — Graph the Inverse of a Function
5-6, Example 4 — Find the Inverse of a Linear Function Using Switch-and-Solve
warmUp: If f(3) = 7, what does this tell us about f^{-1}(7)?
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: switching coordinates; solving for y; verifying inverse by composition; reflection across y = x
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice finding inverses from graphs, tables, ordered pairs, and algebraically using switch-and-solve
independentPractice: Practice problems finding inverses in multiple representations and verifying by composition
assessment: Exit ticket: find the inverse of f(x) = (3x - 7)/2 algebraically and verify it
capReflection: Why does switching x and y in an equation and solving give us the inverse function?
